sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.90 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finitely generated soluble minimax group and let$H$be a finitely generated residually finite group which has precisely the same finite images as$G$. Must$H$be a minimax group? { John S.\,Wilson} Yes, it must (A.\,Mann, D.\,Segal, Proc. London Math. Soc. (3), { 61} (1990), 529--545; see also A.\,V.\,Tushev, Math. Notes, { 56}, no.\,5 (1994), 1190--1192).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
